'''Lynnori''' was a [[Near-Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was the Captain and owner of the ''[[Starbound Misfit]]'', a modified [[YT-1930 transport/Legends|YT-1930 transport]] purchased for her by [[Talandro Starlyte]] and modified by his friend, [[Scizzic]]. She was romantically linked to Talandro and spent a lot of her time on or near [[Starlyte Station]] however she refused to be tied down.

==Biography==
A typical [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] Lynnori lived her days out among the stars traveling from stop to stop moving cargo and passengers around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. She was not opposed to the occasional [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] job as well provided the pay was all right. She became romantically entangled with [[Space station/Legends|station]] owner [[Talandro Starlyte]] who repeatedly requested that she to quit her wandering ways but she repeatedly refused. Concerned for her safety, Talandro ended up purchasing a new [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] and having his associate [[Scizzic]] make some custom modifications so that Lynnori would at least be safer in her travels. He bought her a [[YT-1930 transport/Legends|YT-1930 transport]] that she dubbed the ''[[Starbound Misfit]]''.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Personality and traits==
Lynnori was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[near-Human]]. Her features were feline, with pointed ears, slitted eyes, a long prehensile tail, clawed hands, and a down covering of striped fur. Lynnori was fiercely independent and resisted Talandro's repeated attempts to make her set up a permanent home on [[Starlyte Station]]. She was very physical and loved to flirt, much to the chagrin of Talandro.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Skills and abilities==
A result of living out among the stars was that she had a variety of skills at her disposal. She could operate [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], she could wield [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and repair her ship when it was damaged. She was social and had decent ability with business negotiations and gambling. Finally she had a few clandestine skills as she was able to sneak around unnoticed.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Equipment==
Her pride and joy was the ship that she relied on to take her through the stars. She also wore a [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol]] and a [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka]] [[411 Holdout Blaster]] as well as a [[Vibroknife/Legends|vibroknife]].

==Behind the scenes==
Lynnori was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. She was illustrated by [[Joe Corroney]]. Lynnori's species is given as 'near-Human' however she physically resembles the female [[Farghul]]s [[Breela]] and [[Mika (Farghul)|Mika]]. Farghuls were not created until [[1998]] which was why the species was not listed properly.
